(embedded source map)\n```\nIt is a typical module index file with an inline source map embedded as a data URI.\n\n---\n\n### **Review of Each Report**\n\n#### **Report 1**\n- **Purpose & Sources:** Correctly identifies re-exports and the embedded source map.\n- **Sinks & Flows:** No untrusted data handling or data flow issues.\n- **Anomalies:** Notes the inline source map is unusual but common.\n- **Analysis & Conclusion:** Finds the code benign, standard, and not malicious.\n- **Confidence:** 0.9\n- **Malware & Security Risk:** Both 0.\n\n**Assessment:** Accurate. It is often used for debugging minified or transpiled code. Score should remain **0**.\n\n- **Risk Score:**  \n  The overall security risk is minimal. If source maps are exposed in production unintentionally, it could leak source code, but that is a deployment/configuration concern, not a code security issue.\n\n---\n\n### **Summary**\n\n| Score Type        | Recommended Value | Justification                                              |\n|-------------------|---------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|\n| Malware           | 0                   | No malicious activity detected.                              |\n| Obfuscated        | 0                   | Code is clear and straightforward.                            |\n| Security Risk     | 0.1                 | Minimal risk; embedded source map is benign.                |\n\n**Final note:** The code is a typical, benign module index with an embedded source map, common in build artifacts.
